title: Toyota Motor Corporation FY2025 Financial Results
content: TOYOTA CITY, Japan — Toyota Motor Corporation today announced its financial results for the fiscal year ended March 31, 2025. Net revenues were ¥48.0 trillion (approximately $315 billion at average FY2025 exchange rates), up 9.2% year-over-year. Operating income was ¥4.80 trillion (+3.1%), the third consecutive annual record. Net income attributable to Toyota Motor Corporation shareholders was ¥3.58 trillion. Consolidated vehicle sales volume was 10.87 million units, broadly flat year-over-year as demand normalised from the post-COVID surge. By drivetrain, hybrid vehicles (HEV+PHEV) reached 3.87 million units, representing 36% of total volume. Battery-electric vehicles (BEV) remained a small fraction at 0.39 million units; Toyota has acknowledged that its BEV ramp has lagged peers and is accelerating investment. The bZ4X electric SUV received a significant update in 2025. Capital expenditure was ¥2.7 trillion in FY2025. Research and development expenses were ¥1.2 trillion. Toyota declared an annual cash dividend of ¥125 per share (¥62.5 interim + ¥62.5 year-end), up from ¥110 in FY2024, representing a payout ratio of approximately 38%. President and CEO Koji Sato: 'Toyota's multi-pathway approach — optimising HEV, PHEV, FCEV, and BEV for different markets and use cases — is proving correct. We are accelerating our BEV architecture, software, and battery capabilities to ensure Toyota leads the industry across all powertrains.' Toyota employs 375,000 people globally and manufactures vehicles in 28 countries.
